name: PracticePanther Legal Practice Management API
what_it_does: PracticePanther REST API for cloud-based legal practice management software. Enables AI agents to manage matters and contacts, retrieve time entries and billing data, access payment information, manage tasks and events, and integrate PracticePanther into law firm workflows. PracticePanther is known for its user-friendly interface and competitive pricing for small to mid-size law firms.
use_cases: Retrieving matter and contact data from legal workflow automation agents
Tracking time entries and billing from legal billing automation agents
Accessing payment and invoice status from accounting integration agents
Integrating PracticePanther data into client intake and CRM workflow agents
best_when: A small or mid-size law firm using PracticePanther wants AI agents to automate time tracking, manage billing, and integrate legal data into client communication workflows.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Legal matter data is protected by attorney-client privilege. Handle with extreme care and appropriate access controls.

auth.notes: PracticePanther API uses OAuth 2.0 with authorization code flow. Developer app registration required. User must authorize access to their firm data.

pricing.paid_tiers: Solo, Small Firm, and Business plans; pricing per user per month
pricing.notes: Competitive law firm practice management pricing. Part of Thomson Reuters portfolio.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; not publicly documented

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Attorney-client privileged matter data — highest confidentiality required
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API required
No webhooks — must poll for case and billing status changes
Thomson Reuters ownership — verify API roadmap and long-term support
Consider Clio API for more mature developer ecosystem in legal tech
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Attorney-client privileged data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2 with scopes. Thomson Reuters subsidiary. US data residency.

security_score_components.security_notes: Attorney-client privileged legal data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2 with scopes. US data residency.
